name: judicial_technology_classroom_monitoring_kestrel_jus_mer
premise: A Judicial Technology response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving judicial technology classroom monitoring kestrel jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Elliot Bennett
agents.identity: I am Elliot Bennett, accountable for a defensible call in Judicial Technology when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Elliot Serrano
agents.identity: I am Elliot Serrano, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Judicial Technology.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Elliot Bennett must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Elliot Bennett do?
